name: Morocco
overview: Morocco's Law No. 09-08 on the protection of individuals with regard to the processing of personal data (2009) follows the pre-GDPR French/EU model: declaration and authorisation filings with the CNDP, purpose limitation, consent requirements and transfer authorisations. Morocco ratified Convention 108+ and a modernising reform bill aligning the law with GDPR standards has been in preparation for several years.

regulator.name: Commission Nationale de contrôle de la protection des Données à caractère Personnel (CNDP)

legal_instruments.title: Law No. 09-08 on Protection of Individuals with regard to Processing of Personal Data
legal_instruments.summary: Directive-95/46-style law: prior declaration or authorisation of processing with the CNDP, consent and proportionality requirements, sensitive-data authorisations, data subject rights and criminal penalties.

legal_instruments.title: Decree No. 2-09-165 implementing Law 09-08
legal_instruments.summary: Implementing decree establishing CNDP procedures, declaration/authorisation formats and transfer-authorisation processes.

developments.title: GDPR-alignment reform bill in preparation
developments.summary: The government and CNDP continued work on a draft law replacing 09-08 with a GDPR-style accountability regime, alongside Morocco's Convention 108+ commitments and EU-adequacy ambitions.

developments.title: CNDP campaigns on biometric and CCTV compliance
developments.summary: The CNDP issued deliberations and guidance on facial recognition, CCTV and digital identity processing, requiring prior authorisations.

penalties: Criminal penalties: fines up to MAD 300,000 and imprisonment up to 2 years for offences such as processing without declaration/authorisation or unlawful transfers.
cross_border: Transfers to states without adequate protection require CNDP authorisation; adequacy recognised for states ensuring sufficient protection (CNDP list).
